title: Extract Seam Detection and Stitching Pipeline into Shared Utilities
description: Extract the shortest-path seam detection and stitch instancing pipeline from tool_quilt.py into reusable functions. The pipeline detects cell corners (vertices where both dist_x and dist_y are small), computes shortest edge paths toward corners using dist as edge cost so paths follow seam lines, filters edges with FieldAtIndex to keep only near-seam edges, and converts to curves for stitch/mortar instancing. Also extract the adaptive threshold computation (bounding box → vertex spacing → normalized spacing → threshold) that ensures consistent results regardless of subdivision level or cell scale. For masonry, this same pipeline would generate mortar lines instead of stitches — the seam detection is identical, only the instanced geometry differs.
definition-of-done: 1. Shared function(s) exist for seam detection: corner identification, shortest-path computation, edge filtering, and mesh-to-curve conversion. 2. Adaptive threshold computation is a standalone function taking (group, geometry, subdivisions, scale_x, scale_y) and returning (seam_thresh, vert_spacing, merge_dist). 3. tool_quilt.py uses the shared functions for its stitching. 4. The API is general enough for masonry mortar line generation.
testing-plan: 1. Refactor stitching in tool_quilt.py to use shared pipeline. 2. Verify stitch counts match at scale=0.3, 0.1, 0.05. 3. Validate --group Quilting still passes.
todos: Extract adaptive_threshold(group, geometry, subdivisions, scale_x, scale_y) function
Extract seam_curves(group, geometry, dist, dist_x, dist_y, seam_thresh, merge_dist) function
Refactor tool_quilt.py stitching to use extracted functions
Verify stitch counts are unchanged after refactoring
unknowns: Should the seam_curves function return raw curves or fully instanced stitch/mortar geometry? Returning raw curves is more flexible — the caller handles instancing.
The corner detection threshold uses the same value as the seam threshold. Should these be independently configurable for finer control?
